column1: # katugen template registry — one row per matugen template.
# - `template_relpath` : path under templates/ in this repo.
# - `marker_dir`       : if this dir exists at install time, the template is
#                        enabled. Use ALWAYS for unconditional enable.
#                        ~ is expanded to $HOME at install time.
# - `output_path`      : where matugen writes the generated file. ~ is left
#                        literal; matugen expands it at runtime.
# - `post_hook`        : optional shell command matugen runs after writing the
#                        output. Use NONE for no post-hook. The token
#                        @APPLY@ is substituted with the installed path of
#                        substituted with the installed python helper dir.
#                        Use \ to keep arguments together (matugen splits on
# Lines starting with `#` and blank lines are ignored. Fields are TAB-separated.
# Adapted from noctalia-shell's TemplateRegistry.qml and template-apply.sh.
# Always-on: KDE color scheme + Firefox/pywalfox cache.
# Terminals — output goes in themes/, post-hook wires it into main config.
# Shell prompt — palette block inserted into starship.toml between markers.
# vscode dropped: requires an installed VS Code theme extension and dynamic
# resolution of its install path. Out of scope; install Theme Wallpaper
# Discord — Noctalia ships two themes. Both materials/midnight are written
# for every detected Discord client. Each row has its own marker.
# Spicetify — overwrites the Comfy theme's color.ini (Comfy must be installed).
# Steam (Material-Theme skin must be installed separately)
# Zen Browser — userChrome/userContent staged in cache, auto-imported into
